messages.content:
The Eiffel Tower is a wrought-iron lattice tower located on the Champ de Mars in Paris, France. It was named after the engineer Gustave Eiffel, whose company designed and built the tower. Constructed between 1887 and 1889 as the entrance arch for the 1889 World's Fair (Exposition Universelle), it has become a global cultural icon of France and one of the most recognizable structures in the world.

Standing at about 324 meters (1,063 feet) tall, it was the tallest man-made structure in the world until the completion of the Chrysler Building in New York City in 1930. The Eiffel Tower is one of the most-visited paid monuments in the world, attracting millions of tourists each year. The tower offers three levels for visitors with restaurants, souvenir shops, and viewing platforms providing panoramic views of Paris.
